microsoft_ft: **Collapsed time-to-production** for Azure's resilience validation program by platformizing a 2-person operation into a scalable self-serve system, sustaining 45+ annual drills and driving **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements, self-service scheduling APIs, and deployment configuration surfaces that reduced operational toil by 30% and eliminated engineer dependency for routine drill execution.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing capability, shipping a production-ready drill program in **4 months** with a 94% recovery rate — surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a continuous-validation model adopted across the org.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, coordinating across infrastructure, engineering, and partner teams.

Built an internal **AI agent for drill planning**, restructuring developer-facing workflows and automating planning cycles to cut cycle time by 39%, increase drill capacity by 21%, and free engineers from manual coordination overhead.

Drove partner integration roadmap across Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp — translating deploy-and-operate friction surfaced in customer conversations into self-serve platform features, reducing reliance on dedicated support engineers for production deployments.

Defined and shipped CLI-accessible scheduling and environment-promotion controls for the Resilience Automation Platform, converging previously fragmented authoring surfaces into **one coherent developer path** for staging and production drill configuration.
microsoft_2023: Championed **developer adoption** of AI-driven code generation workflows, running hands-on demos and training sessions across 14 Azure service teams that drove sustained CLI and SDK utilization and saved **37 engineering hours monthly**.

Shaped the product roadmap to include **intent-based YAML generation** — a CLI-first, API-first authoring surface — by conducting 11+ user interviews with Azure service teams to surface critical gaps in the developer experience and model deployment configuration flow.

Improved developer self-serve capability by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool, cutting lookup time by 83% and reducing friction in the model discovery and onboarding path.

Acted as a rigorous researcher and relentless shipper — synthesizing user interview findings into a prioritized feature backlog and driving alignment with engineering leads to move intent-based authoring from concept to roadmap commitment within a single internship cycle.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and cut region launch time by 28% by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework, coordinating 140+ teams around a shared deployment-lifecycle roadmap.

Mobilized a product strategy to eliminate deploy-and-operate friction during region launches — conducting discovery with 20+ service teams to surface **81 hours of manual effort** per region and building a roadmap to automate critical production paths.

Enabled leadership to target automation gaps by engineering a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, turning raw execution data into a prioritization signal for high-impact self-serve infrastructure investments.

Translated customer-reality findings from 20+ service-team interviews into a phased roadmap, establishing a repeatable discovery-to-roadmap process that became the model for cross-org automation planning at Azure's regional scale.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and owning the end-to-end deployment lifecycle from dev through production rollout.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, **prioritizing the developer backlog** to resolve 40+ high-priority tickets and stabilize the engineer intake process for the robotics software platform.

Drove strategic alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, converging fragmented deployment workflows and accelerating the software delivery cycle by 25% — reducing manual steps between staging and production.

Defined reproducible deployment configuration standards for the OS migration, enabling engineering teams to self-serve environment promotion across 1,200+ stations without dedicated support — a model directly analogous to dev→staging→prod promotion controls.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by redesigning the end-to-end developer-of-record workflow — owning scoping, invoicing lifecycle, and CRM configuration for 200+ monthly proposals and eliminating manual handoffs that created conversion friction.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversion rate by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google profile to improve self-serve discovery and first-touch onboarding for new customers.

Improved leadership pricing models by conducting financial profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records**, identifying and sealing margin leaks and delivering a data-driven roadmap for pricing configuration changes.

Owned the full customer journey from first sign-up to closed job — identifying friction in the onboarding and proposal flow, shipping CRM process improvements, and turning operational insights into a prioritized roadmap that compounded across 200+ monthly engagements.

tailoring_notes: 'Collapse time-to-production' (JD outcome framing) woven into microsoft_ft bullet#1 and amazon_robotics bullet#1 to mirror Baseten's primary DevEx goal.

'Deploy-and-operate friction' and 'self-serve deployment' (JD vocabulary) used in microsoft_ft bullet#6, microsoft_2022 bullet#2, and amazon_robotics bullet#3 to reflect Baseten's FDE-to-self-serve growth objective.
'Intent-based YAML' and 'CLI-first / API-first authoring surface' (JD-specific tooling language) surfaced in microsoft_2023 bullet#2 to mirror Baseten's CLI/SDK/config.yml convergence goal.

'Dev→staging→prod environment promotion' and 'reproducible deployments' (JD deployment lifecycle section) woven into amazon_robotics bullet#4 and microsoft_ft bullet#7 to match Baseten's deployment lifecycle ownership requirement.

'Model discovery and onboarding path' (JD first-run experience section) referenced in microsoft_2023 bullet#3 to connect semantic search work to Baseten's Model Library and first-deploy effortlessness goal.
